REPLY MEMO OF P7A IN SUPPORT OF DEFTS MOTION TO DISMISS FILED BY DEFENDANT BAKER PLACES, INC.

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: A R G U E D. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT BAKER PLACES, INC'S MOTION TO DISMISS FOR DELAY IN SERVICE OF SUMMONS IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF DID NOT EFFECTUATE PERSONAL SERVICE ON DEFENDANT'S AGENT FOR SERVICE OF PROCESS. A FRONT DESK RECEPTIONIST IS NOT "A SECRETARY OR ASSISTANT SECRETARY OR OTHER PERSON AUTHORIZED TO ACCEPT SERVICE." (CCP 416.10(B).) PLAINTIFF HAS NOT MET HIS BURDEN TO SHOW THE RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN THE AGENT FOR SERVICE OF PROCESS AND THE PERSON SERVED TO ESTABLISH SUBSTANTIAL COMPLIANCE. DEFENDANT DID NOT WAIVE THE RIGHT TO SEEK DISMISSAL BY FIRST FILING ITS ANSWER TO AVOID ENTRY OF DEFAULT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RICHARD KRAMER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-11-2013 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: No appearance nor objection. Court ordered case dismissed as to DOES 1-10. Proceedings not reported. (610)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-30-2013 Continued To Jul-09-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Delay In Service Of Summons
Off Calendar May-21-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Corie A. Edwards, Esq. of Vogl Meredith Burke, Tel. 925.284.9600, for defendant and moving party;
John F. Henning, III, Esq. specially appearing for plantiff and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT BAKER PLACES, INC'S Motion To Dismiss For Delay In Service Of Summons is granted. Plaintiff did not effectuate personal service on Defendant's Agent for Service of Process. A front desk receptionist is not "a secretary or assistant secretary or other person authorized to accept service." (CCP 416.10(b).) Plaintiff has not met his burden to show the relationship between the Agent for Service of Process and the person served to establish substantial compliance. Defendant did not waive the right to seek dismissal by first filing its Answer to avoid entry of default. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Richard Kramer; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Delay In Service Of Summons
Off Calendar May-07-2013 Continued To May-21-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT BAKER PLACES, INC.'s Motion To Dismiss For Delay In Service Of Summons is Continued to May 21, 2013 at 9:30 am in Dept. 302 to give the opposing party the opportunity to comply with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. The opposing party shall immediately provide a courtesy copy of its opposing papers with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. Judge: Peter J. Busch; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not Reported.

MINI MINUTES: Appearance by Meghan E. Stevens, Esq. for Plaintiff. The Order to Show Cause is re-set to JUN-04-2013 at 10:30 AM in Department 610. Proceedings not reported.
